summary: A single-binary Go HTTP server built with the standard library net/http that exposes a health-check endpoint (GET /healthz) and an echo endpoint (POST /api/echo). The echo endpoint accepts JSON with a message field and returns the message along with its byte length. The server uses no third-party dependencies, includes unit tests via Go's standard testing package, and listens on port 8080 by default.

functional_requirements.title: Health Check Endpoint
functional_requirements.description: The server MUST expose a GET /healthz endpoint that returns a JSON body {"status":"ok"} with HTTP status code 200 and Content-Type application/json.
acceptance_criteria: The server listens on port 8080 by default.
A GET /healthz request returns HTTP 200 with body exactly {"status":"ok"} (no trailing newline).
The response header Content-Type is application/json.
The endpoint responds within 100 ms under normal conditions.

functional_requirements.title: Echo Endpoint
functional_requirements.description: The server MUST expose a POST /api/echo endpoint that accepts a JSON body containing a message field of type string, and returns a JSON body with the echoed message and its length.
acceptance_criteria: The request body MUST be valid JSON with a message key whose value is a string.
If the request body is missing, malformed, or message is absent, the server returns HTTP 400 with {"error":"invalid input"}.
If the message value is not a string type, the server returns HTTP 400 with {"error":"invalid input"}.
On success, the server returns HTTP 200 with body {"echo":"<message>","length":<int>} where <int> is the byte length of the message.
The response header Content-Type is application/json.

functional_requirements.title: Unsupported Methods
functional_requirements.description: The server MUST reject unsupported HTTP methods on known endpoints with HTTP 405 (Method Not Allowed).

functional_requirements.title: Unknown Routes
functional_requirements.description: The server MUST return HTTP 404 (Not Found) for any path that is not a registered endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: Any request to an unregistered path returns 404 with {"error":"not found"}.

functional_requirements.title: Unit Tests
functional_requirements.description: The project MUST include unit tests using Go's standard testing package that cover health check, echo endpoint valid/invalid inputs, unsupported methods, and unknown routes.
acceptance_criteria: Health check endpoint returns correct status code and body.
Echo endpoint with valid input returns correct echo and length.
Echo endpoint with missing message field returns 400.
Echo endpoint with malformed JSON returns 400.
Echo endpoint with non-string message value returns 400.

non_functional_requirements.title: Performance
non_functional_requirements.description: The server MUST handle at least 100 concurrent requests on a single core without exceeding 500 ms response latency (p95). The server MUST start within 2 seconds of execution.

non_functional_requirements.title: Resource Constraints
non_functional_requirements.description: The server MUST NOT allocate more than 32 MB of memory under normal operation. The server MUST NOT depend on any third-party Go modules — only the standard library is allowed.

non_functional_requirements.title: Input Validation
non_functional_requirements.description: The maximum accepted message length is 10 000 bytes. Messages exceeding this limit return HTTP 400 with {"error":"message too long"}.

non_functional_requirements.title: Content-Type Enforcement
non_functional_requirements.description: All responses MUST include the Content-Type: application/json header.

non_functional_requirements.title: Error Response Format
non_functional_requirements.description: All error responses MUST follow the JSON structure {"error":"<reason>"} with an appropriate HTTP status code.

non_functional_requirements.title: Deterministic Output
non_functional_requirements.description: The health check endpoint MUST always return the identical response body {"status":"ok"} with no randomness or timestamps.

use_cases.goal: Verify that the server is running and healthy.
preconditions: The server is started and listening on port 8080.
main_flow: Client parses the response and confirms health.
postconditions: Server continues to serve requests normally.

use_cases.goal: Send a message to the server and receive it back with its length.
preconditions: The server is started and listening on port 8080.
main_flow: Client sends POST /api/echo with JSON body {"message":"hello"}.
Client parses and processes the response.
alternate_flow: If the message is missing or malformed, the server returns 400 Bad Request.
If the message exceeds 10 000 bytes, the server returns 400 Bad Request with {"error":"message too long"}.
postconditions: Server continues to serve requests normally.

use_cases.goal: Attempt an unsupported HTTP method and receive a 405 response.
preconditions: The server is started and listening on port 8080.
main_flow: Server rejects the request with 405 Method Not Allowed.
postconditions: No state is changed on the server.

use_cases.goal: Access an unregistered path and receive a 404 response.
preconditions: The server is started and listening on port 8080.
main_flow: Client sends GET /unknown or any other unregistered path.
Server returns 404 Not Found with {"error":"not found"}.
postconditions: No state is changed on the server.

use_cases.goal: Execute all unit tests using Go's standard testing package and verify they pass.
preconditions: The server code is written and go.mod is initialized.
main_flow: All test cases execute against the in-memory server or handlers.
All tests pass with zero failures.
postconditions: Codebase is verified to meet all acceptance criteria.
